Notes: Pruned back after July bloom. Dried for sachets, tincture base, and two lavender sachets gifted to clients. Bed A1 is sheltered from north wind, handles cold well.
Annual. German variety. Replant from saved seed in spring. Made two full batches of sleep tea blend this season. Flowers dried on screens in apothecary room.

Perennial, third year in bed B1. Root harvest deferred to autumn (October at earliest) per Virginia Keene's notes -- roots need four years for full potency. Aerial parts harvested in August for tincture.
Used fresh in poultry stuffing and dried for immune tea blend. One cutting given to B.W. to start at home. Needs to be divided in spring; plan for the next available gardening window.
Excellent growth this year. Moved one rooted cutting inside in October for winter use on the kitchen windowsill. Three cuttings given to clients.
Container mulched heavily for winter. Harvest was light in second half due to August drought. Plan to add second pot in 2026.
Spearmint and peppermint. Vigorous as always. Kept in contained bed to prevent spreading. Three full harvests. Dried and stored in jars. Also used fresh in herbal water.
Used for poultice preparation only -- always remind clients not to ingest. Leaves harvested twice. Root left undisturbed for regrowth. Bed D2 amended with compost in fall.
